requirements.requirement: The system shall accept a customer account number from either a terminal input or a web-service container and make it available for downstream processing.

chain.role: determine input source and populate the account number
continue processing after input is populated

evidence.code:
EXEC CICS ASSIGN STARTCODE( START-CODE )
END-EXEC.
IF START-CODE = 'TD'
THEN
  MOVE 'Y' TO IS-TERMINAL-BASED
  EXEC CICS RECEIVE INTO     ( READ-INPUT )
                  LENGTH     ( READ-INPUT-LENGTH )
                  NOTRUNCATE
                  RESP       ( COMMAND-RESP )
                  RESP2      ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
  END-EXEC

  MOVE INPUTACCNUM TO CUST-NO-IN
  MOVE INPUTACCNUM TO ACCOUNT-NUM

ELSE
  EXEC CICS GET CONTAINER ('ASYNCPNT' )
                  INTO    ( ACCOUNT-NUMBER-IN )
                  RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                  RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
  END-EXEC
END-IF

ground_truth.rationale: The parent program selects terminal or container input based on the CICS start code and loads the account number into working storage for later use.

requirements.requirement: The system shall place the input account number into a shared CICS container on a shared channel so child programs can read it.

chain.role: publish input to a shared channel and container

evidence.code:
EXEC CICS PUT CONTAINER ( INPUT-CONTAINER )
                FROM    ( ACCOUNT-NUMBER-IN )
                CHANNEL ( MYCHANNEL)
                RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
           END-EXEC

ground_truth.rationale: The parent program explicitly writes the input account number into INPUTCONTAINER on MYCHANNEL before invoking any child service.

requirements.requirement: The system shall start the credit-check, address, customer-status, and customer-name services asynchronously using CICS RUN on the shared channel.

chain.role: fan out to multiple asynchronous child transactions

evidence.code:
EXEC CICS RUN TRANSID      (CREDIT-CHECK-TRAN)
              CHANNEL      (MYCHANNEL)
              CHILD        (CREDIT-CHECK-TKN)
           END-EXEC

EXEC CICS RUN TRANSID      (GET-ADDR-TRAN)
              CHANNEL      (MYCHANNEL)
              CHILD        (GET-ADDR-TKN)
           END-EXEC

EXEC CICS RUN TRANSID      (CSSTATUS-TRAN)
              CHANNEL      (MYCHANNEL)
              CHILD        (CSSTATUS-TKN)
           END-EXEC

EXEC CICS RUN TRANSID      (GET-NAME-TRAN)
              CHANNEL      (MYCHANNEL)
              CHILD        (GET-NAME-TKN)
           END-EXEC

ground_truth.rationale: The parent program fires off four child transactions asynchronously, all sharing MYCHANNEL and distinct child tokens.

requirements.requirement: The system shall wait for the credit-check child to complete before starting the database-cache update child.

chain.role: wait for the credit-check child and only then launch the cache-update child

evidence.code:
EXEC CICS FETCH CHILD       (CREDIT-CHECK-TKN)
                   CHANNEL     (CREDIT-CHECK-CHAN)
                   COMPSTATUS  (CHILD-RETURN-STATUS)
                   ABCODE      (CHILD-RETURN-ABCODE)
           END-EXEC

MOVE '.' TO TRAN5-STATUS
PERFORM PRINT-TEXT-TO-SCREEN

EXEC CICS RUN TRANSID      (DB-CACHE-TRAN)
              CHANNEL      (MYCHANNEL)
              CHILD        (DB-CACHE-TKN)
           END-EXEC

ground_truth.rationale: The parent explicitly fetches the credit-check child first and only then initiates the DB cache child, making the dependency order clear.

requirements.requirement: The system shall retrieve the credit-check result from the child output container and use it in the parent flow.

chain.role: read the credit-check result from the child container

evidence.code:
EXEC CICS PUT CONTAINER ( CRDTCHK-CONTAINER )
                FROM    ( CREDIT-CHECK-RESULT )
                RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
           END-EXEC

EXEC CICS GET CONTAINER (CRDTCHK-CONTAINER)
                   INTO    (CREDIT-CHECK-RESULT)
                   CHANNEL (CREDIT-CHECK-CHAN)
                   RESP    (COMMAND-RESP)
                   RESP2   (COMMAND-RESP2)
           END-EXEC

ground_truth.rationale: The child writes CREDIT-CHECK-RESULT to its container and the parent later reads that same container into its own working storage.

requirements.requirement: The system shall trigger a database-cache update as a separate asynchronous child after the credit-check result is available.

chain.role: launch the cache update as another asynchronous child

evidence.code:

              CHANNEL      (MYCHANNEL)
              CHILD        (DB-CACHE-TKN)
           END-EXEC

ground_truth.rationale: The parent launches UPDCSDB asynchronously and the child program simulates the update with a delay before returning.

populate and return the customer name

           ELSE

EXEC CICS RETURN

requirements.requirement: The system shall show terminal progress by printing status markers for each child transaction as it is started and completed.

emit the progress line to the terminal

evidence.code:
MOVE '.' TO TRAN1-STATUS
           PERFORM PRINT-TEXT-TO-SCREEN

           EXEC CICS RUN TRANSID      (CREDIT-CHECK-TRAN)
                         CHANNEL      (MYCHANNEL)
                         CHILD        (CREDIT-CHECK-TKN)

MOVE 'Y' TO TRAN1-STATUS
           PERFORM PRINT-TEXT-TO-SCREEN

IF IS-TERMINAL-BASED = 'Y' THEN
             EXEC CICS SEND TEXT FROM ( PRINT-LINE )
                       TERMINAL WAIT
                       FREEKB
                       ERASE
             END-EXEC
           END-IF

ground_truth.rationale: The parent toggles progress markers and sends the formatted PRINT-LINE only in terminal-driven execution.

requirements.requirement: The policy-retrieval child shall simulate work by delaying for two seconds before returning.

chain.role: simulate policy retrieval latency and return

requirements.requirement: The spending-retrieval child shall simulate work by delaying for two seconds before returning.

chain.role: simulate spending retrieval latency and return

ground_truth.rationale: GETSPND mirrors GETPOL: it only simulates elapsed time and then returns immediately.

requirements.requirement: The database-cache child shall simulate a three-second cache update before returning control to the parent.

chain.role: simulate the database-cache update and return

ground_truth.rationale: UPDCSDB is a one-way child task that models cache update time with a three-second delay.

requirements.requirement: The sequential parent shall accept the input account number from either terminal input or a web-service container and then create a shared input container.

chain.role: determine input source and populate the account number
place the account number into the shared container

evidence.code:
EXEC CICS ASSIGN STARTCODE( START-CODE )
           END-EXEC
           IF START-CODE = 'TD'
           THEN
             MOVE 'Y' TO IS-TERMINAL-BASED
             EXEC CICS RECEIVE INTO     ( READ-INPUT )
                             LENGTH     ( READ-INPUT-LENGTH )
                             NOTRUNCATE
                             RESP       ( COMMAND-RESP )
                             RESP2      ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
             END-EXEC

ELSE
             EXEC CICS GET CONTAINER ('SEQPNT' )
                             INTO    ( ACCOUNT-NUMBER-IN )
                             RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                             RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
             END-EXEC
           END-IF

EXEC CICS PUT CONTAINER ( INPUT-CONTAINER )
                           FROM    ( ACCOUNT-NUMBER-IN )
                           CHANNEL ( MYCHANNEL)
                           RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                           RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
           END-EXEC

ground_truth.rationale: SEQPNT supports the same two input modes as the asynchronous parent and publishes the account number to a shared channel container.

requirements.requirement: The sequential parent shall invoke the child services in a fixed order, waiting for each child to finish before continuing to the next step.

chain.role: execute the child services in sequence

evidence.code:
EXEC CICS LINK PROGRAM ( CREDIT-CHECK )
                          CHANNEL ( MYCHANNEL )
                          RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                          RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
           END-EXEC

           EXEC CICS GET CONTAINER (CRDTCHK-CONTAINER)
                           INTO    (CREDIT-CHECK-RESULT)

EXEC CICS LINK PROGRAM ( GET-NAME )
                          CHANNEL ( MYCHANNEL )
                          RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                          RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
           END-EXEC

EXEC CICS LINK PROGRAM ( GET-ADDR )
                          CHANNEL ( MYCHANNEL )
                          RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                          RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
           END-EXEC

EXEC CICS LINK PROGRAM ( CSSTATUS )
                          CHANNEL ( MYCHANNEL )
                          RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                          RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
           END-EXEC

EXEC CICS LINK PROGRAM ( DB-CACHE )
                          CHANNEL ( MYCHANNEL )
                          RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                          RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
           END-EXEC

ground_truth.rationale: The sequential parent uses CICS LINK and retrieves each output before moving on, so the flow is strictly ordered rather than parallel.

requirements.requirement: The sequential parent shall assemble the final application result, place it into a return container, and return control to CICS.

chain.role: set the final result and return the complete response structure

requirements.requirement: The system shall provide both asynchronous and sequential orchestration paths for the same credit-card application workflow, producing a completed response in either mode.

evidence.code:
EXEC CICS RUN TRANSID      (CREDIT-CHECK-TRAN)
                         CHANNEL      (MYCHANNEL)
                         CHILD        (CREDIT-CHECK-TKN)
           END-EXEC

      * Finished
      * Summarize the credit card application

           MOVE APPLICATION-SUCCESS TO APPLICATION-RESULT
           MOVE APPLICATION-SUCCESS TO RESULT-TEXT

EXEC CICS LINK PROGRAM ( CREDIT-CHECK )
                          CHANNEL ( MYCHANNEL )
                          RESP    ( COMMAND-RESP )
                          RESP2   ( COMMAND-RESP2 )
           END-EXEC

      * Finished
      * Summarize the credit card application

           MOVE APPLICATION-SUCCESS TO APPLICATION-RESULT
           MOVE APPLICATION-SUCCESS TO RESULT-TEXT

ground_truth.rationale: Both parent programs implement the same business outcome but differ in orchestration style: ASYNCPNT fans out with RUN/FETCH while SEQPNT uses LINK and waits at each step.

requirements.requirement: The sequential status flow shall determine customer importance using the customer's policy holdings and yearly spend rather than account number alone.

chain.role: retrieve input and invoke policy/spend lookups

END-EXEC

END-EXEC

IF ACCOUNT-NUMBER-IN = '0001'

ELSE

END-IF

* and the amount they spent with us in the previous year

ground_truth.rationale: The flow invokes the policy and spend programs, but the actual importance decision is based only on the account number. The intended metric-based calculation is therefore only partially implemented.

issue CICS commands without error handling

* FOR SIMPLICITY OF EXPLANATION, ALL ERROR HANDLING IS REMOVED.

END-IF

ground_truth.rationale: The external service is only simulated. The program chooses between fixed scores and never consumes a real service response.
